Title: grit - Git internals in Rust

URL Source: https://grit-scm.com/

Markdown Content:
## Git,rewritten by agents,in Rust,as a library.

Grit is an agent-driven, library-oriented reimplementation of Git in Rust, aiming to pass Git's own test suite.

[grit-lib](https://crates.io/crates/grit-lib) provides Git behavior as typed Rust modules;

[grit-cli](https://crates.io/crates/grit-cli) is the Git-compatible CLI built to pass Git's own test suite.

$ curl -fsSL https://grit-scm.com/install | sh

$ cargo install grit-cli
$ grit status --short
 M docs/index.html

$ grit log --oneline --decorate -3
cc96ca5 (HEAD, main) gc 8f2d1a0 implement promisor hydrate 5ac721e update index parsing

$ grit diff --stat HEAD~1..HEAD
 grit-lib/src/repo.rs | 42 ++++++++++++++++++++

grit-lib = "0.1"

use grit_lib::repo::Repository;

let repo = Repository::discover(".")?;
let head = grit_lib::state::resolve_head(&repo.git_dir)?;

if let Some(oid) = head.oid() {
    let obj = repo.odb.read(oid)?;
    println!("{} {} bytes", obj.kind, obj.data.len());
}

Current status

## Passing the Git Test Suite

Grit is tracked against the upstream Git harness. The generated dashboard shows pass rate by family, skipped files, and per-file status.

*    140+ Git commands are implemented in the `grit-cli` CLI. 
*    grit-lib covers [objects](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/objects/), [packs](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/pack/), [index](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/index/), [refs](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/refs/), [revisions](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/rev_parse/), [diff](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/diff/), [merge](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/merge_file/), [config](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/config/), [ignore rules](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/ignore/), [hooks](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/hooks/), and [more](https://docs.rs/grit-lib/latest/grit_lib/). 
*    Development is agent-driven, with logs and generated progress checked into the repo. 

[51.6% t0 basics**90.5%** t1 database**43.4%** t2 worktree**55.4%** t3 ls-files**71.1%** t4 diff**47.0%** t5 fetch/push**60.1%** t6 revisions**56.9%** t7 porcelain**58.4%** t8 forensics**24.1%** t9 tools**25.6%** Latest generated harness pass rate: 21,284 of 41,220 in-scope tests. Open the dashboard for exact counts.](https://grit-scm.com/progress/)

Letter from the editor
## Why this exists.

For [over 15 years](https://github.com/libgit2/libgit2/commit/4b7483a285024937df9623c3543390e717e2338a), I've wanted a full implementation of git that was available as a nice, reentrant library. Not a fork-exec'd wrapper around the command line, but a real library that didn't `die` whenever it got scared.

When I read about the Anthropic experiment to build a [C compiler](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-c-compiler) from scratch using agents and test suites, I thought that was a pretty cool idea. Maybe I could do that to make the library of my dreams, in Rust.

So here we are. Grit is the result of that experiment: an agent-driven reimplementation of Git in Rust, built to pass Git's own test suite. Hopefully I can get it to the finish line.

Love, Scott

## What can it do?

Grit already covers the core Git building blocks in both the reusable library and the Git-compatible CLI.

*   [Read and write loose objects](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/odb.rs)
*   [Hash blobs, trees, commits, and tags](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/examples/odb_blob.rs)
*   [Parse object IDs and payloads](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/objects.rs)

*   [Load and write the staging area](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/index.rs)
*   [Add, replace, sort, and refresh entries](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/examples/index_add.rs)
*   [Build trees from the index](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/write_tree.rs)

*   [Read, write, delete, and list refs](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/refs.rs)
*   [Resolve HEAD and symbolic refs](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/state.rs)
*   [Append and expire reflogs](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/reflog.rs)

*   [Parse names like `HEAD~3`](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/rev_parse.rs)
*   [Walk commit graphs](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/examples/rev_list.rs)
*   [Compute merge bases and ancestry](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/merge_base.rs)

*   [Diff trees, index, and worktree](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/diff.rs)
*   [Detect renames and copies](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/diff.rs)
*   [Generate patches and patch IDs](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/patch_ids.rs)

*   [Three-way line merges](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/merge_file.rs)
*   [Conflict styles and merge messages](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/fmt_merge_msg.rs)
*   [Cherry-pick detection helpers](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/examples/cherry_pick.rs)

*   [Load multi-scope Git config](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/config.rs)
*   [Parse typed values](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/src/config.rs)
*   [Match ignore and sparse patterns](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit-lib/examples/ignore_match.rs)

*   [140+ Git commands implemented](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit/src/commands/mod.rs)
*   [Status, log, diff, add, commit, fetch, push](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/grit/src/commands/status.rs)
*   [Harness-tested Git compatibility](https://github.com/schacon/grit/blob/main/scripts/run-tests.sh)

## Try it out.

Install the `grit` CLI and use it as a drop-in replacement for `git`. Pre-built binaries for macOS and Linux, or install via Cargo.

`curl -fsSL https://grit-scm.com/install | sh`

or `cargo install grit-cli`
